title: libc_unsetenv wrapper discards return value — silent failure on env cleanup
description: libc_unsetenv() in both device.rs and connection.rs is declared returning () by discarding the c_int return from unsetenv(3). POSIX unsetenv can fail (EINVAL). In idiomatic Rust, discarding a fallible return value requires explicit acknowledgment. Either propagate the error or document why EINVAL is impossible (the names are always valid ASCII). This is the same class of problem as #[must_use] violation.
